claim: Self-scores become more valuable when the repo can explain which dimensions are structural, operational, or blocked by scope.
mechanism: A raw score is easy to misread. Breaking it into dimensions with explanations about real weaknesses, artifact deficits, and mutable-target constraints helps operators decide whether to patch code, generate evidence, or request broader access. That increases the usefulness of the score because it becomes a planning tool instead of a vanity number.
boundary: Legibility does not replace rigor. Explanations should clarify scores, not rationalize away poor performance.
